item.title: Extract known models list to single source of truth — eliminate duplication between config and blueprint validation
item.intent: Adding a new model to the system must require changing one file, not hunting through multiple hardcoded lists. Model list duplication between blueprint validation and config defaults is a divergence risk.
item.description: The known model list (haiku, sonnet, opus, gpt-4, llama3:8b, etc.) is hardcoded in at least two places: src/design/blueprints/validate.ts and referenced in config validation. Adding a new model requires finding and updating all locations. Extract to a single KNOWN_MODELS constant in src/config/defaults.ts and import it wherever needed.
steps: Add KNOWN_MODELS constant to src/config/defaults.ts with the complete model list
Update src/design/blueprints/validate.ts to import KNOWN_MODELS instead of maintaining its own list
Search for any other hardcoded model lists in the codebase and replace with imports
Add test: KNOWN_MODELS contains all expected model families (haiku, sonnet, opus, gpt-4*, llama3*, mistral*, phi3*, gemma*, qwen2*)
Verify all existing validation tests still pass
acceptanceCriteria: A single KNOWN_MODELS constant exists in src/config/defaults.ts
src/design/blueprints/validate.ts imports and uses KNOWN_MODELS instead of a local list
No other file in src/ defines its own model list that duplicates KNOWN_MODELS
